task_completion.description: Verify the vintage computer CSV data has been imported into a spreadsheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open displaying columns of data for vintage computers, including models like Apple II Plus, Commodore 64, and IBM PCjr.

task_completion.description: Verify that incomplete entries from the imported data have been completed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the row for the 'Sinclair ZX Spectrum'. The year column for this entry must contain a valid year (e.g., 1982) and should no longer be blank.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a multi-factor restoration priority scoring system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new column exists (e.g., 'Restoration Priority', 'Score', or similar) that contains numerical values. These values should clearly be derived from multiple other columns (like rarity, condition, and parts availability).

task_completion.description: Verify conditional formatting is applied to the spreadsheet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells in the spreadsheet (most likely in the priority score column) display automatic formatting such as background color scales (e.g., red/yellow/green), data bars, or colored text based on their values.

task_completion.description: Verify the collection is sorted by the restoration priority.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rows in the spreadsheet are ordered sequentially (either strictly ascending or strictly descending) based on the values in the restoration priority score column.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a genuine GUI spreadsheet application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface of a spreadsheet program (like LibreOffice Calc) is visible. The final result should not just be a text file viewed in an editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't use terminal commands to bypass GUI spreadsheet manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing bash, python, awk, or sed commands used to manipulate the CSV file directly, calculate scores, or sort the data.

integrity.description: Verify the scoring system uses spreadsheet formulas rather than hardcoded text.
integrity.visual_evidence: If a cell in the priority score column is selected in any screenshot, the formula bar at the top should show a mathematical formula (e.g., using +, *, or functions) rather than a static typed number.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The imported data contains 10 vintage computer models: Apple II Plus, Commodore 64, IBM PCjr, TRS-80 Model III, Atari 800XL, Sinclair ZX Spectrum, Amiga 500, Kaypro II, Apple IIe, and TI-99/4A. The original CSV intentionally had a missing year for the 'Sinclair ZX Spectrum', which the agent must fill in to satisfy the 'completed entries' requirement.
